content.text: Answer four problems. Write your answers clearly in complete English sentences.

body.text: Denote by \(Q_8\) the quaternion group. Show that if \(f:Q_8\to S_n\) is an injective homomorphism then \(n\geq 8\).

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: Show that if \(G\) is abelian, the subset \(H\) of elements of finite order is a subgroup.

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: Give an example of a (nonabelian) group \(G\) and elements of finite order \(x,y\in G\) such that \(xy\) has infinite order, and prove that this example is correct.

body.text: Prove the first assertion of Sylow’s theorem: if \(G\) is a group of order \(n\), \(p\) is prime and \(p^a\) is the largest power of \(p\) dividing \(n\), then \(G\) has a subgroup of order \(p^a\). State clearly any preliminary results you need.

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: Define what it means for a group \(G\) to be nilpotent.

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: Show that any nilpotent group is solvable.

subparts.label: (iii)
subparts.text: Give an example of a finite solvable group that is not nilpotent, and prove that this example is correct.

body.text: Let \(G\) be a group acting on a set \(S\), and \(H\subseteq G\) a normal subgroup. Assume that for any \(x_1,x_2\in S\) there is a unique \(h\in H\) such that \(h(x_1)=x_2\). For \(x\in S\) let \(G_x\) be the stabilizer of \(x\). Show that for any \(x\in S\), \(G\) is a semi-direct product of \(G_x\) and \(H\). Do not assume that \(G\) is finite.
In both cases, justify your answer.

subparts.text: Find all conjugacy classes of elements of order \(2\) in \(S_6\).

body.text: Show that the normalizer of a \(5\)-Sylow subgroup of \(A_5\) has order \(10\), and is a maximal subgroup of \(A_5\).
